item.title: DOJ: Estrada, Bonoan plunder raps on flood control mess submitted for resolution
item.description: The plunder and graft complaints filed by the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) against Sen. Jinggoy Estrada and former Public Works Secretary Manuel Bonoan were already submitted for resolution, the Department of Justice (DOJ) said on Thursday.

item.title: Elago files complaint for disciplinary action vs. lawyer
item.description: Gabriela party-list Rep. Sarah Elago has filed a complaint for disciplinary action, which may lead to disbarment, against a lawyer she refused to name on Thursday before the Integrated Bar of the Philippines (IBP) Commission on Bar Discipline.

item.title: Sara Duterte may be liable for nonfeasance, breach of public trust, Palace says
item.description: Duterte has claimed that there were already indications of a looming war as early as 2022, and criticized the Marcos Jr. administration for supposedly not acting swiftly to brace for its possible impacts.

item.title: Dizon eyes completion of unfinished PUP buildings by 2027 to address classroom shortage
item.description: Public Works Secretary Vince Dizon on Thursday committed to allocate P1.5 billion in the agency’s 2027 budget to finally complete long-stalled academic and administrative buildings across several Polytechnic University of the Philippines (PUP) campuses nationwide.

item.title: Senators blast BFAR official for skipping hearing
item.description: Senators reprimanded BFAR National Director Elizer Salilig for failing to attend the February 18 hearing of the Committee on Agriculture, Food and Agrarian Reform where commercial fishing within 15-kilometer municipal waters was discussed, among other matters.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
